## Types of Imperfect Feedback {.nav-feedback}

Human feedback is not always perfect. There exist three distinct kinds of imperfections

<table>
<thead><tr><th>Type</th><th>Definition</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="fragment"><td>**Inconsistency** [@inconsistent]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}</td><td>New feedback contradicts previous feedback in the same session.</td></tr>
<tr class="fragment"><td>**Suboptimality** [@mindmeld, @ird, @occam]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}</td><td>Provided demonstrations consistently do not perfectly optimize the underlying reward $R$.</td></tr>
<tr class="fragment"><td>**Heterogeneity** [@mindmeld, @occam]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}</td><td>Feedback is influenced by the style, skill-set, and priorities of the particular demonstrator.</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- Each of these imperfections manifest in *systematic*, *non-random* ways. -->

<!-- ::: {.fragment} -->
<div style="margin-top: -0.5em; text-align: center;">
Reward learning methods, like IRL and PBL, must account for these imperfections to accurately recover the true reward $R$.
</div>
<!-- ::: -->
